zh.Bramford, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可布拉福德

nameMeanings.name: Hughes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Hugh with genitival or excrescent -s.
Irish and Scottish: adopted as an equivalent of Gaelic surnames based on the personal name Aodh ‘fire’, for example Ó hAodha, Mac Aodha; see McCoy and compare McHugh .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes was born about 1500, in Bramford, Suffolk, England. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Treathone. She died in 1568, in her hometown, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes est née environ 1500, à Bramford, Suffolk, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Thomas Treathone. Elle est décédée en 1568, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes wurde um 1500 in Bramford, Suffolk, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Thomas Treathone mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1568 im Alter von 69 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes è nata il circa 1500 nel Bramford, Suffolk, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Thomas Treathone. Morì nel 1568, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.Bramford, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクブラムフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughesは約1500年にイギリススフォルクブラムフォードで生まれた。彼女はThomas Treathoneとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1568年に故郷で69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes(은)는 대략 1500년 영국 수폴크 브램퍼드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Treathone(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1568년 그녀의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes nasceu aproximadamente 1500, em Bramford, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Thomas Treathone. Ela faleceu em 1568, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes родилась около 1500 г. в местности брамфорд, суффолк, Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Thomas Treathone. Она умерла в 1568 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Hughes nació aproximadamente 1500, en Bramford, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Thomas Treathone. Murió en 1568, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años.
